etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 朝(朝) (cháo) -> tide
etymology.interpretation: The character 潮 combines the semantic element 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water"), suggesting water-related concepts, with the phonetic element 朝(朝) (cháo), which provides the sound. The semantic component clearly indicates its association with water, leading to meanings related to tides, waves, and currents.

examples.chinese: 海水的潮起潮落每天都在变化(海水的潮起潮落每天都在變化)。
examples.pinyin: Hǎishuǐ de cháo qǐ cháo luò měitiān dōu zài biànhuà.
examples.english: The rise and fall of the tide changes every day.

examples.chinese: 早晨的空气有点潮，地上也湿了(早晨的空氣有點潮，地上也濕了)。
examples.pinyin: Zǎochén de kōngqì yǒudiǎn cháo, dìshàng yě shī le.
examples.english: The morning air is a bit moist, and the ground is wet.

examples.chinese: 科技潮流推动着社会的进步(科技潮流推动着社会的進步)。
examples.pinyin: Kējì cháoliú tuīdòngzhe shèhuì de jìnbù.
examples.english: The wave of technology drives societal progress.
